questions.q: How many players from each team play on the court at one time in basketball?
questions.explanation: Five players from each team play on the court at once in basketball: typically 2 guards, 2 forwards, and 1 center. Having 4 or 6 would violate standard basketball rules.

questions.q: What is the height of a basketball hoop above the ground?
questions.explanation: A basketball hoop is exactly 10 feet (3.05 meters) above the ground. This standard height has been used since basketball's invention in 1891 and applies across professional, college, and most recreational leagues.

questions.q: How many points is a three-point shot worth in basketball?
questions.explanation: A three-point shot is worth three points when a player shoots from beyond the three-point line. A regular field goal from inside the line is worth two points, and a free throw is worth one point.

questions.q: What is the diameter of a basketball court?
questions.explanation: A standard basketball court is 94 feet long and 50 feet wide. The three-point line is 23 feet 9 inches from the basket, and the court dimensions are consistent across professional and college basketball.

questions.q: In basketball, what violation occurs when a player continues dribbling after stopping?
questions.explanation: Double dribble occurs when a player dribbles the ball with two hands simultaneously or resumes dribbling after stopping. Traveling is when a player moves without dribbling; these are distinct violations.

questions.q: How much time does a team have to shoot the ball in professional basketball?
questions.explanation: In the NBA (National Basketball Association), teams have 24 seconds to attempt a shot that hits the rim. In college basketball and FIBA, the shot clock is 30 seconds, showing different standards across leagues.

questions.q: What does a 'pick and roll' refer to in basketball?
options: An offensive play where one player screens for another who rolls toward the basket
A violation resulting in a technical foul
questions.explanation: A pick and roll is an offensive play where one player sets a screen (pick) for a teammate with the ball, then rolls toward the basket for a potential pass. It is one of the most fundamental and effective offensive plays in basketball.

questions.q: How many fouls can a player commit before fouling out of a basketball game?
questions.explanation: A player fouls out after committing 6 personal fouls in professional basketball. In college basketball, players foul out after 5 fouls. After fouling out, the player must leave the game.

questions.q: What is the area painted in front of the basket where offensive players can only stay for 3 seconds called?
questions.explanation: All three terms are correct: this rectangular area is called the restricted zone, the key (because of its keyhole shape), or the paint (because it is painted a distinct color). Players cannot remain there longer than 3 seconds without the ball.

questions.q: What penalty is assessed for a technical foul in basketball?
options: One free throw and possession of the ball
questions.explanation: A technical foul results in one free throw and possession of the ball for the opposing team. Technical fouls are called for unsporting conduct, arguing with officials, or bench violations. Multiple technicals can lead to ejection.
